consumer_purpose: Every agent that calls smart_find gets file listings with zero binary dependencies
claim_atoms: Native walker works on every machine with zero external dependencies
fd is optional accelerator — tried first, native always works
No error when fd is absent — native walker just works silently
warning field only if fd was available but failed (binary found, execution failed)
Remove 'brew install fd' error message entirely
Basic .gitignore parsing: walk up directories, parse patterns, match entries
Backward compatible output shape: same args, same success output, backend may say 'native'

root_causes.hypothesis: No native fallback exists — fd binary dependency is treated as mandatory
evidence: Lines 91-98: spawnFd() failure returns {status:'fail', error:'fd not found. Install with: brew install fd'} with no fallback logic
Lines 35-47: spawnFd() tries multiple binary paths but when all fail, returns the error result without distinguishing 'not found' from 'crashed'
The file has no readdirSync import and no recursive walking logic anywhere in the codebase

root_causes.hypothesis: Binary-first design pattern is the convention across all .opencode/tools/
evidence: All 30 tool files use spawnSync-based external binary calls; none implement native fallbacks

root_causes.hypothesis: .gitignore handling was a design constraint favoring fd
evidence: Description line 51: 'respects .gitignore' listed as a reason for using fd
No .gitignore parsing code exists anywhere in .opencode/tools/

fixes.description: Add matchGlob() — glob-to-regex converter ported from core smart-find.ts
fixes.before: // (no glob matching function exists — fd handles all pattern matching)
risks: Regex injection: glob pattern could contain malformed regex. Mitigated by try/catch fallback
validation: matchGlob('foo.ts', '*.ts') === true
matchGlob('foo.js', '*.ts') === false
matchGlob('foo', 'f?o') === true

risks: Negation patterns (!) are skipped — could cause false positives (files incorrectly ignored)
Globstar (**) not supported — deep path matching won't work
Nested .gitignore precedence: parent+child patterns both apply; real git has child override parent
validation: Create .gitignore with '*.log', verify .log files are skipped
Verify node_modules directory is skipped via root .gitignore
Verify .DS_Store files are skipped

fixes.description: Rewrite spawnFd → tryFd: differentiate 'fd not found' (return null) from 'fd found but failed' (return result + warning)
risks: ENOENT detection via error.message string match — fragile if Node.js changes error message format. Mitigation: falls through to native anyway
validation: When fd not installed: tryFd returns {result:null, binary:null, warning:null}
When fd installed and works: returns {result, binary, warning:null}
When fd installed but crashes: returns {result, binary, warning:'...'}

fixes.description: Add walkNative() — synchronous recursive directory walker using readdirSync({withFileTypes:true}) + lazy statSync
fixes.before: // (no native walker — fd handles all traversal)
risks: Deep recursion: theoretical stack overflow on >10K-deep directory trees (real-world: <100 levels, safe)
Large directories: readdirSync loads all entries into memory — 100K+ files per directory could use GBs. Mitigation: rare in practice; fd would also struggle
Symlink following: all symlinks skipped entirely — user may expect symlinked files to appear. Mitigation: documented; install fd for symlink following
Performance vs fd: native walker is ~5-20x slower than fd (single-threaded, no caching). Acceptable for fallback path
Bun compatibility: readdirSync({withFileTypes:true}) with Dirent.isFile()/isDirectory() works in Bun 1.x+
validation: Walk a directory with known files, verify all files found
Verify maxResults truncation
Verify maxDepth enforcement
Verify type filter (file/directory/both)
Verify pattern matching with glob
Verify newerThanMs filtering with statSync
Verify includeSizes adds size_bytes field

fixes.description: Rewrite execute() — try fd first, fall back to native, remove failure-only output path
fixes.before: Lines 64-155: try fd → if fail return error → else parse fd output
fixes.after: Lines 64-~120: tryFd → if fd success parse fd output → else walkNative → build unified output with backend field
risks: Output consumers checking status==='fail' will no longer trigger — no consumers found
command field absent when native — informational field, not parsed by consumers
validation: Run with fd present → backend:'fd', command present
Run without fd → backend:'native', no error, no command
Run with broken fd (chmod -x /opt/homebrew/bin/fd) → backend:'native', warning present

fixes.description: Update tool description string to reflect native-primary + fd-optional architecture
fixes.before: "Find files and directories. Uses fd (Rust) for speed — respects .gitignore, 5-20x faster than find. Returns file info with sizes, modified times, and counts."
fixes.after: "Find files and directories. Native Node.js walker with basic .gitignore support; optionally accelerates with fd (Rust) when available. Returns file info with sizes, modified times, and counts."
validation: Verify description renders correctly in tool help

section_order: Helper functions (unchanged): resolvePath, hb, artifactLog, analytics

changed_fields: backend: now 'native' or fd path (was always fd path)
added_fields: warning (only when fd found but failed)
conditional_fields: command: only present when backend is not 'native'

edge_cases_handled: EACCES on directory → silently skipped (try/catch around readdirSync)
Missing files during statSync → skipped (try/catch continue)
Empty directories → returns count:0, no error
Pattern with no matches → returns count:0, no error
Path not found → returns existing error (unchanged from current)
maxResults=0 → returns empty results immediately (guard clause)
.gitignore with CRLF line endings → split('\n') handles it; trim() removes \r
.gitignore at nested levels → walked from searchPath up to rootDir
Unicode filenames → Node.js fs handles natively

validation_plan.pre_baseline: In an environment without fd: verify current behavior returns error. In an environment with fd: capture baseline output for a known directory tree.
smoke_tests: No pattern, no filter → finds everything in directory
include_sizes=true → size_bytes field present per file
fd_integration: With fd: backend shows fd path, command field present
Without fd: backend is 'native', no warning
With broken fd: backend is 'native', warning field present
regression: All existing smart_find calls in agent markdown files still work (same arg names)
Permission config in opencode.jsonc unchanged (tool name unchanged)

subagent_findings_pending: risk-enumerator: detailed risk assessment with severity ratings
root-cause-analyst: git history analysis for original design decisions
